2605.21318.title: TextReg: Mitigating Prompt Distributional Overfitting via Regularized Text-Space Optimization
2605.21318.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are highly sensitive to the prompts used to specify task objectives and behavioral constraints. Many recent prompt optimization methods iteratively rewrite prompts using LLM-generated feedback, but the resulting prompts often become longer, accumulate narrow sample-specific rules, and generalize poorly beyond the training distribution. We study this failure mode as prompt distributional overfitting and argue that it reflects a lack of representation control in discrete text-space optimization. We formalize this view through representational inefficiency, a dual-factor measure that decomposes prompt inefficiency into capacity cost and scope narrowness, attributing distributional prompt overfitting to their coupled growth during optimization. We propose TextReg, a regularization framework that realizes a soft-penalty objective through regularized textual gradients, combining Dual-Evidence Gradient Purification, Semantic Edit Regularization, and Regularization-Guided Prompt Update. Across multiple reasoning benchmarks, TextReg substantially improves out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization, with accuracy gains of up to +11.8% over TextGrad and +16.5% over REVOLVE.
2605.21318.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）對於用來指定任務目標和行為約束的提示非常敏感。許多最近的提示優化方法通過使用LLM生成的反饋來迭代重寫提示，但結果提示往往變得更長，累積狹窄的樣本特定規則，並且在訓練分佈之外的泛化效果不佳。我們將這種失敗模式研究為提示分佈過擬合，並認為這反映了在離散文本空間優化中缺乏表徵控制。我們通過表徵低效性來形式化這一觀點，這是一種雙因素度量，將提示低效性分解為容量成本和範圍狹窄，將分佈提示過擬合歸因於它們在優化過程中的耦合增長。我們提出了TextReg，一個正則化框架，通過正則化的文本梯度實現軟懲罰目標，結合了雙證據梯度純化、語義編輯正則化和正則化引導的提示更新。在多個推理基準上，TextReg顯著改善了分佈外（OOD）泛化，準確率相比TextGrad提高了最多11.8%，相比REVOLVE提高了最多16.5%。
2605.21318.author: Lucheng Fu et.al.
2605.21318.authors: Lucheng Fu, Ye Yu, Yiyang Wang, Yiqiao Jin, Haibo Jin, B. Aditya Prakash, Haohan Wang
